vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup passwd File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/passwd-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/passwd-

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes file mode bits on a backup copy of /etc/passwd. This does not affect running authentication services, boot process, or networking, and follows standard STIG file‑permission hardening.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Improper permissions could expose password hashes if the backup file is accessed; Incorrect chmod syntax could leave the file overly permissive
